title: Group for Answers dependant on Results
excerpt: I have a board with multiple groups I am managing, and I am creating forms to assist in collecting data. I notice the setting of “group for answers” when creating a form which will determine which group the data will be …

posts.author: tomhayes1994 (Tom Hayes)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a board with multiple groups I am managing, and I am creating forms to assist in collecting data. I notice the setting of “group for answers” when creating a form which will determine which group the data will be sent to once the form is complete however I am trying to limit the number of forms created for simplicity.

I am creating a list of contacts in a board with each group being seperated by state (QLD, NSW, VIC). Is it possible to create one form which would give a dropdown of which state the contact belongs at the start which would then determine the group the data would be assisned to? Or do I need to make a seperate form for each state as each form can only go to one group?

Appreciate the help.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can do it with one. You’d then set an automation that says ‘when item is created, if state is X, move to group X.’. You’ll need to duplicate that automation for each state. (Unless you have the workflow center, then you can use branches to specify where it moves to).

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Tom,

You can definitely use a single form to collect data and assign form responses to different groups based on a dropdown choice like state.

Here’s how you can do it:

  • On your form, include a dropdown question for the state (QLD, NSW, VIC).

  • Set the form’s “Group for answers” to a default group (any group).

  • Then, set up automations in your board:

    • “When item is created, if state is QLD, move item to QLD group”

    • Repeat for NSW, VIC, etc.
      This way, one form handles all states, and automations route the items to the correct group based on the dropdown answer.

Note: If you have monday’s Workflow Center (available on some plans), you can use conditional branches in automations to make this more streamlined and efficient.

Desiree McCarthy’s advice is spot on about duplicating automations per state or using Workflow Center for advanced routing.

Helpful resource on WorkForms settings and “Group for answers”:
https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/11040473466258-WorkForms-settings-and-permissions
